summary: Pristine digital soundboard captures a mid‑90s set highlighted by an epic Playin>Drumz>Wharf Rat jam and stellar Throwing Stones, though the first set is marred by weak Easy Answers and Broken Arrow.
review: The recording is a crystal‑clear digital SBD that lets every guitar line, keyboard, and drum be heard distinctly. Musically the show shines in the second half – the Playin>Drumz>Wharf Rat sequence, Throwing Stones, Wharf Rat and the rare Rain cover are repeatedly praised – but the first set stalls with Easy Answers, Broken Arrow and a tired Jerry vocal on some Beatles numbers.

ai_rating.rationale: Recording quality is unanimously described as excellent (soundboard, crystal‑clear), which scores high. Show quality is generally strong with several standout jams, but recurring criticism of a few songs drags the overall musical rating down, yielding an overall 4‑star assessment.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Digital SBD is described as crystal‑clear with all three guitars distinct; no dropouts or muddiness reported.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set opens strong with Shakedown but loses momentum in the middle and ends on weaker choices (Easy Answers, Broken Arrow). The second set builds dramatically to a massive Playin jam and closes powerfully, delivering several high points despite occasional vocal wear.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His grizzled voice adds character to Wharf Rat but some reviewers note his vocals sounded tired on the Beatles numbers.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bobby (Bob Weir) is singled out for outstanding playing throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keyboards are noted as clear and well‑balanced in the mix, contributing to the overall crisp sound.
band_member_comments.Drums: The Drumz segment receives high praise; reviewers love the precise, percussion‑heavy jam and even mention ambient insect sounds adding texture.
